questions.question: Which statement best describes Mean?
questions.option_a: Average value of a dataset
questions.option_c: Higher specificity reduces false positives.
questions.option_d: Range of plausible values for a parameter

questions.option_c: Middle value when data are ordered

questions.question: Which statement best describes Median?

questions.option_b: Middle value when data are ordered

questions.option_d: Range of plausible values for a parameter
questions.explanation: Robust to outliers; useful for skewed distributions.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Standard deviation (SD)?

questions.explanation: Larger SD indicates more spread.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Standard error (SE)?
questions.option_a: Larger SD indicates more spread.
questions.option_c: Average value of a dataset

questions.question: In Mathematics / Biostatistics, Standard error (SE) is MOST associated with:

questions.option_d: Range of plausible values for a parameter

questions.question: Which statement best describes Confidence interval (CI)?

questions.explanation: Narrow CI indicates higher precision.

questions.option_c: Narrow CI indicates higher precision.
